treatment_information.dosage_or_details: complete right spheno-ethmoidectomy, middle turbinectomy, access septoplasty, right endoscopic medial maxillectomy, excision en bloc along with partial resection of the orbital floor, medial orbital wall and surrounding mucosa of the maxillary sinus
treatment_information.treatment_response: The patient’s serum sodium level subsequently returned to normal after removal of the tumour. Excision margins were clear but microinvasion of the orbital floor bone marrow was identified.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with a 1-year history of lethargy, nausea and pre-syncopal symptoms. Routine blood investigations revealed significant hyponatraemia, with a serum sodium level of 111 mmol/L.
MRI performed primarily to investigate for pituitary pathology. Demonstrated a normal pituitary but an incidental right maxillary sinus high-signal intensity lesion.
CT of the paranasal sinuses demonstrated a soft tissue density lesion within the right maxillary sinus with areas of presumed calcification.
PET scan confirmed a high-activity lesion with no regional or distant disease.
Examination under anaesthesia demonstrated a pedunculated lesion arising from the roof of the maxillary sinus. Endoscopic biopsy was performed.
Histology demonstrated a Hyam’s grade 1 esthesioneuroblastoma. The lesion was staged as Kadish B and Dulgerov T1N0M0.
Surgical resection with adjuvant radiotherapy was recommended.
Endoscopic resection performed. The lesion was excised en bloc along with partial resection of the orbital floor, medial orbital wall and surrounding mucosa of the maxillary sinus.
Adjuvant intensity-modulated radiation therapy of 50 Gy was administered.

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: followed up every 6 months for the first 3 years, then yearly thereafter. At each follow-up visit, thorough flexible nasendoscopic examination of the patent sinus cavities and MRI was performed to assess for recurrence.
patient_outcome.complications: No postoperative morbidity, such as hypoglobus, has been experienced by the patient.
